mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: ollama gpt-oss not working
mission.description: [Bug]: ollama gpt-oss not working. This issue affects 16+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

```
Type:
APIConnectionError
Message:
litellm.APIConnectionError: Unable to parse ollama chunk - {'model': 'gpt-oss:20b', 'created_at': '2025-08-06T14:34:31.5276077Z', 'response': '', 'thinking': 'User', 'done': False}
Traceback

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: ollama gpt-oss not working
steps.description:
### Problem
Ollama GPT-OSS models were failing with `APIConnectionError: Unable to parse ollama chunk` when streaming responses contained a `'thinking'` field with empty `'response'`. The chunk parser didn't handle this specific case, causing streaming to fail.
```yaml
Type:
APIConnectionError
Message:
litellm.APIConnectionError: Unable to parse ollama chunk - {'model': 'gpt-oss:20b', 'created_at': '2025-08-06T14:34:31.5276077Z', 'response': '', 'thinking': 'User', 'done': False}
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/litellm_core_utils/streaming_handler.py", line 1677, in __anext__
    async for chunk in self.completion_stream:
    ...<51 lines>...
        return processed_chunk
  File "/usr/lib/python3.13/site-packages/litellm/llms/base_llm/base_model_iterator.py", line 128, in __anext__
    chunk = self._handl
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: ollama gpt-oss not working is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
### Problem
Ollama GPT-OSS models were failing with `APIConnectionError: Unable to parse ollama chunk` when streaming responses contained a `'thinking'` field with empty `'response'`. The chunk parser didn't handle this specific case, causing streaming to fail.

model_list:
  - model_name: "my-thinking-model"
    litellm_params:
      model: "ollama/gpt-oss:120b"
      api_base: "http://localhost:11434"

litellm_settings:
  disable_auth: True
  # This tells litellm to find the my_stream_modifier function
  # inside the my_callbacks.py file.
  stream_response: "my_callbacks.my_stream_modifier"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
